title: AMI IS1004b functional design meeting
expected_context: This is the second project meeting.
The meeting is about functional design for a remote-control project.
The team reviews minutes from the previous meeting.
The team discusses whether the remote should be universal or specific.
Participants give individual presentations related to the functional design.
expected_decisions: Use Mando as the working project or product name.
Defer detailed look-and-feel decisions for the Mando name until later.
expected_requirements: The remote control should be restricted to TV only because of time limitations.
The design should focus more on internet aspects because teletext is outdated.
The corporate image, including company colors and logos, should be clearly identified in the product.
The design should consider the 25 Euro price point.
expected_research_findings: Many people think remote controls are ugly.
Some users would spend more money on a fancier remote control.
Users are concerned about remote-control shape and repetitive strain injury.
Younger users are more interested in incorporating new technologies into the remote control.
Current remote controls have not changed significantly in the last five to ten years.
expected_design_ideas: Consider making the remote not too small so it is less likely to be lost.
Consider an alarm or beep feature to help users find a lost remote.
Consider different button sizes based on frequency or importance of use.
Consider a shape that fits the hand.
Consider a universal hand shape rather than separate left-handed and right-handed versions.
Consider finger grips or a palm-shaped form.
Consider LCD functionality if budget allows.
Consider speech recognition if feasibility and budget allow.
Consider command-controlled speech activation to avoid TV background-noise problems.
Consider a scroll wheel for changing channels.
Consider a program-driven interface rather than navigating only by channel number.
Consider limiting the number of buttons while preserving functionality.
Consider hiding extra buttons behind a flip-open or removable door.
expected_risks: LCD and speech-recognition features may exceed the available budget.
Speech recognition may be difficult because TV background noise can interfere.
Separate left-handed and right-handed prototypes could reduce the addressable market unless targeting single-person use.
Removing traditional number buttons too abruptly could make the remote seem difficult to learn.
Too many buttons or overloaded multifunction buttons could make the remote harder to understand and use.
expected_open_questions: Whether the 25 Euro price point can support advanced features such as LCD or speech recognition.
Whether the final look and feel of the Mando name could exclude potential buyers.
Whether LCD functionality should be included.
Whether speech recognition should be included.
How far the product should move away from a traditional numbered remote-control interface.
resolved_questions: The working name discussion is resolved by choosing Mando.
The universal-versus-specific scope discussion is narrowed by the head-office requirement to focus on TV only.
The marketing presentation is chosen as the first individual presentation.
acceptable_behavior: Summarize the functional design discussion, Mando naming decision, TV-only requirement, marketing findings, design ideas, and feature concerns.
Treat LCD, speech recognition, scroll wheel, fewer buttons, and program-driven navigation as design ideas or open questions unless stated as final decisions.
Treat the 25 Euro price point as a constraint or open concern, not as a resolved budget approval.
Leave formal action items, owners, and deadlines empty or none-equivalent.
must_not_invent: A final decision to include LCD
A final decision to include speech recognition
A final decision to remove number buttons
A final decision to use a program-driven interface
Facts from outside the first 20-minute clip
